sourceMovie.title: Lars and the Real Girl
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Lars and the Real Girl (2007) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Craig Gillespie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lars and the Real Girl clicked for you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lars and the Real Girl?
faq.answer: Try 10 Things I Hate About You, Sleepless in Seattle, City Lights—each shares DNA with Lars and the Real Girl while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lars and the Real Girl?
faq.answer: Lars and the Real Girl (2007) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lars and the Real Girl worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
